name: Kentucky Department of Corrections — Division of Reentry Services
description: The Kentucky Department of Corrections Division of Reentry Services coordinates statewide reentry planning for people in state custody or under community supervision. The division oversees 13 Reentry Service Centers across Kentucky and assigns regional reentry coordinators who work with individuals and families before and after release. They help build individualized reentry plans and connect people to housing, employment, benefits, and community partners. This is a planning and referral office—not a crisis or emergency line.
eligibility: Varies by program—generally serves Kentucky residents under DOC custody, supervision, or seeking state reentry services.
notes: For your area, contact the regional Reentry Coordinator listed at corrections.ky.gov/Reentry.

name: CTS-Russell (Community Transitional Services)
description: Kentucky DOC-contracted Reentry Service Center for men in community custody, operated by Community Transitional Services (CTS). Provides licensed transitional housing plus structured programming: Moral Reconation Therapy (MRT), employment preparation, substance use education, and case management aligned with parole and probation requirements. Admissions require a referral from Kentucky DOC Probation and Parole—call to confirm current bed availability.
eligibility: Provides licensed transitional housing plus structured programming: Moral Reconation Therapy (MRT), employment preparation, substance use education, and case management aligned with parole and probation requirements. Admissions require a referral from Kentucky DOC Probation and Parole—call to confirm current bed availability.
notes: Male DOC-contracted Reentry Service Center offering transitional housing, employment preparation, MRT, and reentry programming.
hours: Visitation Saturday–Sunday, 12:00–3:00 p.m.; call for office hours

name: Kentucky Career Center — Lexington (Bluegrass)
description: Kentucky Career Center – Bluegrass is the American Job Center serving 17 Central Kentucky counties including Fayette, Madison, Jessamine, and Woodford. Free services include job search assistance, resume workshops, career assessment, veterans' services, youth programs, and referrals to training and credentialing. Staff help justice-involved job seekers connect to employment opportunities and barrier-removal resources.
eligibility: Open to all job seekers, including those recently released from incarceration. Core career services are free.
notes: Serves Anderson, Bourbon, Boyle, Clark, Estill, Fayette, Franklin, Garrard, Harrison, Jessamine, Lincoln, Madison, Mercer, Nicholas, Powell, Scott, and Woodford counties.

name: kynect — Medicaid & Benefits Enrollment
description: kynect is Kentucky's official online portal to apply for Medicaid (including the Kentucky HEALTH plan), SNAP food benefits, KTAP cash assistance, childcare subsidies, and other public benefits. People leaving incarceration can enroll in Medicaid immediately upon release—and under Kentucky's federal 1115 Reentry waiver, eligible individuals may receive up to one year of Medicaid coverage plus pre-release screening starting 60 days before release. Apply online, call the helpline, or visit any DCBS office.
eligibility: Open to Kentucky residents in the service area. Medicaid, Medicare, and most insurance accepted; crisis services available regardless of ability to pay.
notes: Medicaid reentry waiver: pre-release screening up to 60 days before release For local DCBS office, visit kynect.ky.gov or call 1-855-306-8959.
hours: Online 24/7; phone support Monday–Friday during business hours

name: Stepworks Recovery Centers
description: Stepworks Recovery Centers operates six Kentucky locations offering medical detox, residential (ASAM 3.5/3.7), and outpatient substance use treatment. Facilities in Elizabethtown, Bowling Green, Nicholasville, London, and Paducah. Medical detox for opioids, alcohol, and benzodiazepines; accepts most insurance including Medicaid. Single statewide access line for referrals and admissions.
eligibility: Adults seeking substance use treatment. Medicaid and private insurance accepted at many locations; call for intake and admission criteria.
notes: Statewide access center: (800) 545-9031. London location is men-only residential. Pregnant women accepted with OB approval. See stepworks.com/insurance for accepted plans.

name: Kentucky Legal Aid — Bowling Green
description: Kentucky Legal Aid serves low-income residents in 35 western and south-central Kentucky counties including Warren, Daviess, Christian, and Hopkins. Free civil legal help with housing, public benefits, family matters, and expungement when records create employment or housing barriers. Statewide intake line routes callers to the nearest office in Bowling Green, Owensboro, or Paducah.
eligibility: Low-income Kentucky residents. Intake required to confirm income eligibility and whether your legal issue is covered.
hours: Intake line Mon–Fri during business hours

name: Kentucky SNAP Enrollment — kynect
description: Apply for SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program/food stamps) through Kentucky's kynect portal, by phone, or at any DCBS office in all 120 counties. Kentucky has lifted the lifetime ban on SNAP for people with drug-related felony convictions—you may remain eligible if you meet requirements under KRS 205.2005. Benefits loaded on EBT card. Also use kynect for Medicaid and other benefits in one application.
eligibility: Kentucky has lifted the lifetime ban on SNAP for people with drug-related felony convictions—you may remain eligible if you meet requirements under KRS 205.2005.
notes: Apply at any county DCBS office or online. Same kynect portal as Medicaid and KTAP.
hours: Online 24/7; DCBS offices Mon–Fri business hours

name: Social Security Administration — Locate an Office
description: Use SSA's national office locator to find the nearest Social Security field office anywhere in Kentucky. A Social Security number and card (or SSA verification letter) is required to obtain a Kentucky driver's license or state ID and for most employment. Call 1-800-772-1213 for automated services and appointment scheduling, or visit ssa.gov for online card replacement and benefit applications. As of 2025, in-person field office visits generally require an appointment.
eligibility: Kentucky residents. Returning citizens with felony convictions may use a Felon Release Letter plus certified birth certificate for a standard ID—see program details.
notes: National toll-free: 1-800-772-1213 (Mon–Fri 8 a.m.–7 p.m.). TTY: 1-800-325-0778. Online services at ssa.gov/myaccount for many card and benefit needs.
hours: National phone line Mon–Fri 8:00 a.m.–7:00 p.m.; field office hours vary by location

name: LIHEAP — Utility Assistance (Community Action Kentucky)
description: Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP) helps eligible Kentucky households pay heating, cooling, and crisis utility bills. Administered through local Community Action Agencies in all 120 counties via Community Action Kentucky (CAPKY). Three components: Subsidy (seasonal, typically November–December), Crisis (heating emergencies, January–March), and Summer Subsidy (cooling, July–September). Income at or below 150% of Federal Poverty Guidelines. Apply at your county's Community Action office—call 1-800-456-3452 or use capky.org/network to find your local agency.
eligibility: Low-income individuals and families; income guidelines apply for most programs. Criminal history is generally not a barrier.
notes: Call to find your local Community Action office
hours: Local agency hours vary; statewide info line during business hours

name: TANK — Transit Authority of Northern Kentucky
description: Transit Authority of Northern Kentucky (TANK) provides public bus service in Boone, Campbell, and Kenton counties with connections to downtown Cincinnati. Operates 27 routes, 19 park-and-ride locations, and the RAMP paratransit program for riders with disabilities, 365 days a year. Covington Transit Center on Madison Avenue is a major hub. Essential transportation for NKY residents without vehicles traveling to work, appointments, and Cincinnati-area destinations.
eligibility: Open to the public; reduced fares may require eligibility documentation (age, disability, or income).
notes: Lost & found and customer service: 859-331-TANK (8265), Mon–Fri 7 a.m.–6 p.m. Schedules and fare info at tankbus.org.
hours: Route hours vary by line; administration Mon–Fri 7:00 a.m.–6:00 p.m.

name: KCTCS — Adult Education & GED (Statewide)
description: Kentucky Community and Technical College System (KCTCS) administers free adult education and GED preparation statewide through 13 college-based Kentucky Adult Education (KYAE) providers in all 120 counties. Classes offered in communities, jails, and prisons. Kentucky pays for first GED test in each subject (plus limited retakes) for eligible test-takers who pass the free GED Ready practice test—use promo code KYFREEGED1. Over 4,460 GEDs awarded to incarcerated Kentuckians 2020–2024.
eligibility: Kentucky residents age 18 or older. All adult education and GED services are free through KYAE.
notes: Contact your local KCTCS college to find the nearest program
hours: Local center hours vary by KCTCS college—contact nearest provider

name: National Call Center for Homeless Veterans
description: National Call Center for Homeless Veterans (877-4AID-VET) is a free, confidential 24/7 hotline connecting veterans— including those leaving incarceration without housing—to the nearest VA homeless program, emergency shelter, and case management. Critical first contact for veterans with no place to stay after release. Trained counselors assess needs and link to HUD-VASH, SSVF, and other VA housing resources. The program supports Kentuckians navigating reentry in throughout Kentucky.
eligibility: U.S. military veterans and eligible family members. Some programs serve veterans with justice involvement.
notes: Also chat at va.gov/homeless/nationalcallcenter.asp. Have DD-214 and SSN ready if available. Works with local VA Medical Center homeless teams.
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week

name: KVC Kentucky — Family Reunification Services
description: KVC Kentucky is a nonprofit child welfare and behavioral health organization headquartered in Lexington, serving more than 22,000 children and families annually across eight Kentucky regions. Family First Preservation and Reunification Services help families stay together or reunify after out-of-home placement through in-home, evidence-based support coordinated with DCBS. Also provides behavioral health, substance abuse treatment, and foster care services. All preservation/reunification referrals come through the Department for Community Based Services (DCBS)—contact your DCBS worker to initiate. The program supports Kentuckians navigating reentry in throughout Kentucky.
eligibility: Varies by program—open to parents, caregivers, and families affected by incarceration.
notes: For current intake, call (859) 254-1035; visit https://kentucky.kvc.org/. Monday–Friday during business hours; behavioral health intake by appointment.
hours: Monday–Friday during business hours; behavioral health intake by appointment

name: Veterans Crisis Line
description: Veterans Crisis Line provides free, confidential crisis support for veterans and their families 24/7. Call or text 988 then press 1, or chat at veteranscrisisline.net. Trained responders—many veterans themselves—provide immediate support for suicidal crisis, PTSD episodes, and reentry stress. Also supports active duty, Guard, Reserve, and family members.
eligibility: U.S. military veterans and eligible family members. Some programs serve veterans with justice involvement.
notes: Text 838255 to connect. If immediate danger, call 911. Spanish-language support available.
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week

name: Gateway Community & Technical College — Adult Education
description: Gateway Community & Technical College Adult Education offers free GED preparation in Northern Kentucky for Boone, Kenton, Campbell, Grant, Owen, and surrounding counties. Kentucky waives GED testing fees for first-time test takers working through a KYAE center. Classes at Florence campus and community sites. Gateway also recognizes GED College Ready scores for college enrollment.
eligibility: Kentucky residents age 18 or older. All adult education and GED services are free through KYAE.
notes: GED fee waiver available when working with local Gateway/KYAE office.

name: Kentucky Adult Education — Find a Program
description: Kentucky Adult Education (KYAE) is the state office connecting residents to free adult education in all 120 counties—GED preparation, literacy, ESL, workforce certifications, and family literacy. Use the online locator to find your county's KCTCS-based adult education center. All GED seekers must pass the GED Ready practice test (free at KYAE centers) before qualifying for state-funded GED testing.
eligibility: Kentucky residents age 18 or older. All adult education and GED services are free through KYAE.
notes: Promo codes: KYFREEGED1 (test center) or OPKYFREEGED (online proctored).
hours: Varies by county center—use locator for local contact

name: Ashland Community & Technical College — Adult Education
description: Ashland Community & Technical College (ACTC) Adult Education provides free GED preparation and adult literacy for Boyd County and surrounding Northeast Kentucky counties. Part of the KCTCS/KYAE statewide network with classes in community and county jail settings. Kentucky waives first-time GED test fees for eligible students passing GED Ready at a KYAE center. The program supports Kentuckians navigating reentry in 10 Kentucky counties.
eligibility: Kentucky waives first-time GED test fees for eligible students passing GED Ready at a KYAE center.
notes: Call (606) 326-2000 for enrollment. Promo code KYFREEGED1 for state-funded testing. Also offers ESL and workforce certifications.
hours: Monday–Friday during business hours; class schedules vary

name: Big Sandy Community & Technical College — Adult Education
description: Big Sandy Community & Technical College — Adult Education serves justice-involved adults and families rebuilding stability after incarceration in 7 Kentucky counties. Big Sandy Community & Technical College (BSCTC) Adult Education offers free GED classes for Floyd, Johnson, Lawrence, Magoffin, Martin, and Pike counties. Part of KCTCS/KYAE with instruction inside Big Sandy Regional Detention Center and Pike County Detention Center—critical for Eastern Kentucky reentry education. Coalition and navigator programs connect returning citizens to local partners—they rarely provide emergency shelter, cash aid, or 24/7 crisis response on their own. Contact this resource to confirm current intake steps, required documents, and whether walk-ins or referrals are accepted.
eligibility: Kentucky residents age 18 or older. All adult education and GED services are free through KYAE.
notes: Prestonsburg campus: One Bert T. Combs Drive. Call (606) 886-3863 for enrollment.

name: Somerset Community College — Adult Education
description: Somerset Community College — Adult Education serves justice-involved adults and families rebuilding stability after incarceration in 10 Kentucky counties. Somerset Community College (SCC) Adult Education provides free GED preparation and adult education for Pulaski County and the Lake Cumberland region. KCTCS/KYAE network member serving adults in community settings and county jails. State-funded first-time GED testing for eligible students. Coalition and navigator programs connect returning citizens to local partners—they rarely provide emergency shelter, cash aid, or 24/7 crisis response on their own. Contact this resource to confirm current intake steps, required documents, and whether walk-ins or referrals are accepted.
eligibility: State-funded first-time GED testing for eligible students.
notes: For current intake, call (606) 679-8501; visit https://somerset.kctcs.edu/workforce-solutions/adult-education/index.aspx. Monday–Friday during business hours.

name: Owensboro Community & Technical College — Adult Education
description: Owensboro Community & Technical College — Adult Education serves justice-involved adults and families rebuilding stability after incarceration in 7 Kentucky counties. Owensboro Community & Technical College (OCTC) Adult Education offers free GED preparation and adult literacy for Daviess County and surrounding Green River counties. KCTCS/KYAE provider with classes at community locations and Daviess County Detention Center. First-time GED test fees waived for KYAE-eligible students. Coalition and navigator programs connect returning citizens to local partners—they rarely provide emergency shelter, cash aid, or 24/7 crisis response on their own. Contact this resource to confirm current intake steps, required documents, and whether walk-ins or referrals are accepted.
eligibility: First-time GED test fees waived for KYAE-eligible students.
notes: Call (270) 686-4400 for enrollment. GED Ready free at KYAE center. Also offers ESL and workplace certifications.

name: Madisonville Community College — Adult Education
description: Madisonville Community College (MCC) Adult Education (ACE²) provides free GED preparation, adult literacy, ESL, and workforce skills for Hopkins and Muhlenberg counties through Kentucky Adult Education (KYAE). Classes meet at the North Campus John H. Gray Building with instruction also available in county jails including Hopkins County and Muhlenberg County detention centers. First-time GED testing fees waived for eligible KYAE students.
address: 2000 College Drive, John H. Gray Building, Rooms 210-213
eligibility: First-time GED testing fees waived for eligible KYAE students.
notes: Enter via Glema Mahr Center concourse, third floor, then stairs/elevator to second floor near Assessment Center. Also serves Muhlenberg County. Promo code KYFREEGED1 for state-funded testing.

name: Hazard Community & Technical College — Adult Education
description: Hazard Community and Technical College (HCTC) Adult Education provides free GED preparation and adult education for Perry, Breathitt, Knott, Leslie, Letcher, Lee, Owsley, and Harlan counties. KYAE provider with in-person and virtual classes at the Devert Owens Technical Center and GED instruction inside Perry County Detention Center and regional county jails. First-time GED test fees waived for eligible students.
address: 101 Vo-Tech Drive, Devert Owens Technical Center, Room 112
eligibility: First-time GED test fees waived for eligible students.
notes: Virtual classes Tuesday and Friday. Email hctc-adulted@kctcs.edu. Serves eight coalfield counties—call for nearest satellite location.
hours: Monday–Friday, 8:00 a.m.–4:30 p.m.; in-person classes Mon–Fri

name: Elizabethtown Community & Technical College — Adult Education
description: Elizabethtown Community and Technical College (ECTC) hosts the Heartland Adult Education Consortium lead center, providing free GED preparation, ESL, citizenship, and college-readiness for Hardin, LaRue, Breckinridge, Grayson, and Hart counties. KYAE provider with in-person and virtual instruction plus corrections education at Hardin County Detention Center. All enrollment, tutoring, and graduation services are free.
eligibility: Kentucky residents age 18 or older. All adult education and GED services are free through KYAE.
notes: Heartland consortium also has centers in Hardinsburg, Leitchfield, and Munfordville. GED testing at ECTC Assessment Center RPC Room 129. Radcliff satellite for northern Hardin County.
hours: Monday–Friday during business hours; virtual and evening options available

name: Maysville Community & Technical College — Adult Education
description: Maysville Community and Technical College (MCTC) Adult Education provides free GED preparation, literacy, ESL, and college-readiness for Mason, Bath, Bracken, Fleming, Nicholas, Robertson, Harrison, and Lewis counties. KYAE provider with flexible in-person and online scheduling at the Maysville campus and instruction in county jail settings. Kentucky covers GED testing costs for eligible students. The program supports Kentuckians navigating reentry in 10 Kentucky counties.
address: 1755 US Highway 68, Administration Building, Room A307
eligibility: Kentucky residents age 18 or older. All adult education and GED services are free through KYAE.
notes: Alternate line (606) 301-6049. Evening and weekend classes at multiple sites. Family literacy programming available.

name: Hopkinsville Community College — Adult Education
description: Hopkinsville Community College (HCC) Adult Education provides free GED preparation, adult literacy, and college-transition support for Christian and Todd counties through KYAE. Main center at the Technology Building with satellite classes in Oak Grove and Elkton. Offers corrections education inside Christian County Detention Center plus GED Plus pathways pairing GED with certificate programs.
eligibility: Kentucky residents age 18 or older. All adult education and GED services are free through KYAE.
notes: Email hp-adulted@kctcs.edu. Oak Grove: 1705 Thompsonville Lane (Mon/Wed mornings). Todd County Library Elkton Tue/Thu mornings. Accelerated GED week-long courses available—pre-register required.
hours: Monday–Friday, 8:00 a.m.–4:30 p.m.; Monday evenings 5:00–7:00 p.m.

name: kynect — KTAP & Financial Assistance
description: kynect is Kentucky's online benefits portal for applying for KTAP (Kentucky Transitional Assistance Program) cash assistance, SNAP food benefits, Medicaid, Child Care Assistance Program (CCAP), and other programs. KTAP provides monthly cash aid to low-income families with dependent children, with a 60-month lifetime limit, plus work support through Kentucky Works. Apply online at kynect.ky.gov, by phone at 855-306-8959, or in person at any DCBS office in all 120 counties. Use the prescreening tool before applying to check potential eligibility.
eligibility: Low-income individuals and families; income guidelines apply for most programs. Criminal history is generally not a barrier.
notes: DCBS Call Services: 855-306-8959 (Mon–Fri 8 a.m.–5 p.m. ET).
hours: Phone line Monday–Friday, 8:00 a.m.–5:00 p.m. ET; online applications 24/7

name: 211 Kentucky — Crisis Resource Navigation
description: Kentucky 211 connects residents to local emergency financial assistance, food, housing, utilities, mental health services, and other community resources. Dial 2-1-1 from any phone or text your zip code to 898211 to reach a trained specialist 24 hours a day, 7 days a week. Service provided through United Way of Kentucky's network covering all 120 counties. Free, confidential, and available in multiple languages with interpreter support. Also searchable online at kentucky211.org.
eligibility: Low-income individuals and families; income guidelines apply for most programs. Criminal history is generally not a barrier.
notes: Text zip code to 898211 (no hyphen). 211 does not provide direct cash assistance—specialists refer to local programs. Pairs with kynect.ky.gov for benefits applications.
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week

name: NKCAC — Warsaw Office (Gallatin & Surrounding Counties)
description: Northern Kentucky Community Action Commission (NKCAC) Gallatin County Neighborhood Center in Warsaw provides LIHEAP utility assistance, weatherization, emergency aid, food security programs, and kynect benefits enrollment for Gallatin, Carroll, Grant, and Owen counties. Serves low-income individuals regardless of criminal history; reentry clients encouraged to apply for needs-based assistance.
eligibility: Serves low-income individuals regardless of criminal history
notes: Neighborhood Center hours Mon–Fri 12:00–4:00 p.m. some services Mon–Thu 7:30 a.m.–5:00 p.m. per city listing. Virtual appointments available 11 a.m.–12 p.m. Mon–Fri. NKCAC headquarters: 717 Madison Ave, Covington.

name: Bluegrass Community Action Partnership — Boyle County
description: Bluegrass Community Action Partnership (BGCAP) Boyle County office in Danville provides LIHEAP utility assistance, weatherization, emergency financial aid, kynect benefits enrollment, and family support for low-income residents of Boyle and Garrard counties. Serves individuals regardless of criminal history; official kynector for Medicaid, SNAP, and health plan applications. The program supports Kentuckians navigating reentry in Boyle, and Garrard counties.
eligibility: Low-income individuals and families; income guidelines apply for most programs. Criminal history is generally not a barrier.
notes: Kynector Julie Turpin: (502) 232-4456. Also serves Garrard County from Danville office. LIHEAP seasonal—apply early when program opens.

name: Bluegrass Community Action Partnership — Lincoln County
description: Bluegrass Community Action Partnership (BGCAP) Lincoln County office in Stanford provides LIHEAP utility assistance, weatherization, emergency financial aid, food referrals, and kynect benefits enrollment for low-income Lincoln County residents. Serves clients regardless of criminal history; walk-ins welcome—call ahead to confirm hours and seasonal program availability. The program supports Kentuckians navigating reentry in Lincoln County.
eligibility: Low-income individuals and families; income guidelines apply for most programs. Criminal history is generally not a barrier.
notes: Office hours 7:30 a.m.–4:00 p.m. per BGCAP listing. Kynector Michelle Ballew: (502) 682-0944. Part of nine-county BGCAP service area.

name: DCBS — Bath County Family Support
description: DCBS — Bath County Family Support serves justice-involved adults and families rebuilding stability after incarceration in Bath County. DCBS Family Support office in Owingsville helps Bath County residents apply for Medicaid, SNAP, KTAP, and child care assistance through kynect. Returning citizens and families rebuilding after incarceration can establish health coverage and food benefits at this county office or online with local staff assisting document verification. Coalition and navigator programs connect returning citizens to local partners—they rarely provide emergency shelter, cash aid, or 24/7 crisis response on their own. Contact this resource to confirm current intake steps, required documents, and whether walk-ins or referrals are accepted.
eligibility: Bath County residents meeting income and household-size requirements for Medicaid, SNAP, KTAP, and child care assistance; criminal record generally not a barrier.
notes: Apply online at kynect.ky.gov or visit this county DCBS office; bring ID, Social Security card, and release documents if applicable.

name: DCBS — Montgomery County Family Support
description: DCBS — Montgomery County Family Support serves justice-involved adults and families rebuilding stability after incarceration in Montgomery County. DCBS Family Support office in Mount Sterling administers Medicaid, SNAP, KTAP, and child care subsidies for Montgomery County residents including justice-involved individuals reconnecting to public benefits after release from Montgomery County Detention Center or state custody. Coalition and navigator programs connect returning citizens to local partners—they rarely provide emergency shelter, cash aid, or 24/7 crisis response on their own. Contact this resource to confirm current intake steps, required documents, and whether walk-ins or referrals are accepted.
eligibility: Montgomery County residents meeting income and household-size requirements for Medicaid, SNAP, KTAP, and child care assistance; criminal record generally not a barrier.
notes: Apply online at kynect.ky.gov or visit this county DCBS office; bring ID, Social Security card, and release documents if applicable.

name: DCBS — Rowan County Family Support
description: DCBS — Rowan County Family Support serves justice-involved adults and families rebuilding stability after incarceration in Rowan County. DCBS Family Support office in Morehead helps Rowan County and northeast Kentucky residents apply for Medicaid, SNAP, KTAP, and child care through kynect. Returning citizens can establish food and health coverage after release with in-person assistance at this county DCBS location. Coalition and navigator programs connect returning citizens to local partners—they rarely provide emergency shelter, cash aid, or 24/7 crisis response on their own. Contact this resource to confirm current intake steps, required documents, and whether walk-ins or referrals are accepted.
eligibility: Rowan County residents meeting income and household-size requirements for Medicaid, SNAP, KTAP, and child care assistance; criminal record generally not a barrier.
notes: Apply online at kynect.ky.gov or visit this county DCBS office; bring ID, Social Security card, and release documents if applicable.

name: DCBS — Robertson County Family Support
description: DCBS — Robertson County Family Support serves justice-involved adults and families rebuilding stability after incarceration in Robertson County. DCBS Family Support office in Mount Olivet is the county benefits intake point for Robertson County residents applying for Medicaid, SNAP, KTAP, and child care assistance. Justice-involved residents can apply online at kynect.ky.gov or visit this office for help with verification after release from local jail or state facilities. Coalition and navigator programs connect returning citizens to local partners—they rarely provide emergency shelter, cash aid, or 24/7 crisis response on their own. Contact this resource to confirm current intake steps, required documents, and whether walk-ins or referrals are accepted.
eligibility: Robertson County residents meeting income and household-size requirements for Medicaid, SNAP, KTAP, and child care assistance; criminal record generally not a barrier.
notes: Apply online at kynect.ky.gov or visit this county DCBS office; bring ID, Social Security card, and release documents if applicable.
